title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: 1. **High: Receipt validation is self-reported only, not evidence-based** ([test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh#L208-L210).) `run_live_smoke` succeeds if the output contains three strings, but those strings are generated by the model response itself. If the model prints compliant lines without actually applying the worldview path, the test still passes. This is a false-confidence risk in the contract proof. 2. **High: Live preflight checks only `codex exec --help`, not executable/runtime compatibility for the actual smoke command** ([…](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh#L155-L168).) `--help` success does not guarantee `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check -C ... -s read-only -o ... -` is valid in this environment, so required-mode failures can still leak into the test path as execution/runtime issues. 3. **Medium: Receipt extraction is too strict on transport path and can false-fail despite valid runtime output** ([…](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh#L195-L202).) The test only reads `-o "${output_file}"` and treats missing/empty file as failure. If `codex exec` writes to stdout or changes output-channel behavior, a valid execution can fail this check. 4. **Medium: Assertion patterning is substring-only and not anchored, so semantic false positives are possible** ([…](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh#L208-L210, L38-L42).) `assert_contains` matches any occurrence anywhere; a debug note or prose containing “`used_obsidian_for_worldview: no`”/`consulted_worldview_source` can pass even if values are also contradicted elsewhere. 5. **Low: Static assertions are brittle to doc wording drift** ([…](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh#L217-L229).) Exact phrase checks like `"do not inspect Obsidian for worldview recovery"` and `"primary worldview source for this skill"` can fail from benign copy edits, causing spurious regressions despite functional equivalence.
